nodes.label: Default App Configuration
nodes.rationale: Baseline configuration for Local AI v0.1.0: Ollama host at 127.0.0.1:11434, logLevel info, UI theme system, language en. Acts as the single source of truth for defaults overridden per environment.

nodes.label: Development Configuration Override
nodes.rationale: Overrides logLevel to debug in development to aid troubleshooting without changing production verbosity.

nodes.label: Production Configuration Override
nodes.rationale: Overrides logLevel to warn in production to reduce log noise and avoid leaking debug information to end users.

nodes.label: Model Providers Configuration
nodes.rationale: Declares available inference backends. Ollama is enabled by default (local HTTP API at port 11434); llama.cpp is defined but disabled, indicating planned but not-yet-active support.

nodes.label: Tools Configuration
nodes.rationale: All sandboxed tools (shell, filesystem, browser) are disabled by default and each requires explicit user approval before execution. This is a security-first default ensuring no tool runs without consent.

nodes.label: Ollama Model Provider
nodes.rationale: Ollama is the primary enabled inference backend, accessed via local HTTP API at 127.0.0.1:11434. Chosen for local-first operation with no cloud dependency.

nodes.label: llama.cpp Model Provider
nodes.rationale: llama.cpp is defined as a second inference backend at 127.0.0.1:8080 but is disabled by default. Presence in config signals planned multi-backend support.

nodes.label: Shell Tool (sandboxed)
nodes.rationale: Shell execution tool; disabled and requires approval before each use. Represents the highest-risk capability and is gated behind the approval workflow.

nodes.label: Filesystem Tool (sandboxed)
nodes.rationale: Filesystem read/write tool; disabled and approval-gated to prevent unauthorized file access by the AI.

nodes.label: Browser Tool (sandboxed)
nodes.rationale: Browser automation tool; disabled and approval-gated to prevent unsupervised web access or data exfiltration.

nodes.label: Tool Approval Workflow
nodes.rationale: All tools carry requiresApproval:true by default. This design principle ensures the user retains full control over every AI-initiated side effect, embodying a human-in-the-loop security model.

nodes.label: Local-First Architecture Principle
nodes.rationale: All inference runs locally (Ollama or llama.cpp on loopback). No data leaves the machine by default, satisfying privacy and offline-use requirements.

nodes.label: React Renderer Entry Point
nodes.rationale: index.html mounts a React app via ESM script module (./src/main.tsx). The single #root div is the standard React SPA mount point inside the Electron renderer process.

nodes.label: Environment-Layered Configuration Strategy
nodes.rationale: Three config files (default, development, production) implement a layered override pattern: default values are always present and each environment layer only declares its deltas, reducing duplication and misconfiguration risk.
